def: adj. (f. -ī), Clean, pure, unadulterated, genuine; neat, good, choice, excellent; honest; clever, dexterous; pleasing, delightful, beautiful; fine, sharp, high (as a tone or note, &c.); sharp, keen, pungent.

ety: Prk. चोक्खो and चोक्खओ; S. चोक्षः and चोक्ष+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Clean, pure, unadulterated, genuine; neat, good, choice, excellent; honest; clever, dexterous; pleasing, delightful, beautiful; fine, sharp, high (as a tone or note, &c.); sharp, keen, pungent.
more: adj. See achchhā , 4—7, 10, 12, 16, 24. 1. Kharī mazdūrī, chokhā kām . Prov. Cash payment, work excellent. 2. Ek to lottā hai, dūsrā kahe chokhī de . Prov. One is already on the floor, another says brew it strong. (The alehouse). 3. Mūrakh se kyā kahiye, jaṛ se kyā bausāë? Pāhan meṅ ke mārbe, chokho tīr nasāë . Kabīr. To a blockhead words are vain, with a fool what can you do? Against a stone your arrows shattered all you'll rue.
n. m. A station where four boundaries meet.
adj. Pure, unadulterated, genuine, neat, good, choice, fine, sharp.

def: s.f. Purity, genuineness, &c.; fineness, sharpness, pungency.
s.m. A station where four boundaries meet.

ety: ćokhā+ān = Prk. अअणं = अत्तणं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं); and ˚āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका
def: s.f. Purity, genuineness, &c.; fineness, sharpness, pungency.
s.m. A short jacket, a bodice; the body of a gown; a gown, coat, cloak; a garment worn by a bride (at her marriage); — case, mould, form, frame; the body; animal life, being
s.f. A small jacket, a bodice; a waistcoat; body (of a gown or coat); — a small betel-basket
more: n. f. S. चोली A boddice ( aṅgiyā ); the body of a coat. cholī-panth , n. m. A new free sect of Panjābīs who permit promiscuous intercourse of the sexes. cholī dāman kā sāth. lit . attached as the body to the skirt. A close attachment or friendship. Hindū Musalmān kā cholī dāman kā sāth hai . Prov. Hindus and Musalmen close knit as skirt with body close doth fit.
s. f. 1. A bodice, a waistcoat, the body of a gown, a jacket. 2. A small betel-basket.
subs: (-kā), 'To be as closely united as the body and skirt (of a garment), to be united in close friendship, to be on very good terms.

def: s.m. Short breeches, reaching half-way down the thighs.
more: n. m. Short breeches reaching down to the knee ( kāchh ).
s. (dakh.) Short breeches reaching only half way down the thighs: see H. چرنا charnā, and کاچهـ kāchh.

Hold your tongue ( zabān saṅbhālo )! be silent! enough! choṅch mārnā , v. a. 1. To peck. 2. To talk at. choṅch milānā , v. n. To bill and coo. do do choṅheṅ h. , W.; do do choṅch kī h. , E. v. n. To exchange a few angry words; to have a tiff.

def: v.t. To peck.

subs: (or safaid) honā, To become grey-headed, the hair to become white (with age)
To have the head shaved; to suffer disgrace, be disgraced
'To whiten one's hair in the sun,' to be grey-headed without experience or wisdom, to pass (one's) life in vain.

ety: contract. from चोंगला, fr. S. चतुर्+अङ्गुल+कं
def: s.m. 'A hand-breadth'; a joint of bamboo (closed at one end, used to send letters in, or to keep oil, salt, &c. in); a tube; barrel (of a gun); a funnel; a drill; a cylindrical tin case; — a looped strap, &c.
more: n. m. Wheedling; cajolery; allurement; bait. chauṅgā k. , v. a. To wheedle; cajole.

n. m. 1. (Sant. ṭhongā ) A piece of hollow bamboo closed at one end, used for keeping oil, salt, letters, etc.; a drill; a cylindrical tin case. 2. A strap formed into a loop; the leather strap which supports the fore end of the traces. 3. Birds' food. Ausakh māṅ chiṛī ke choṅge ḍhāl khān sūṅ . W. Rus. The sick man feeds like a bird.
s. m. Wheedling. چونگا کرنا chaungā karnā, To wheedle out of money.

def: s.m. = ćogā, q.v. s.m. A kind of cloak like a morning-gown (made of wool or camel's hair).
s.f. dim. of ćoṅgā, q.v.

s.m. A joint of bamboo, &c. ( = ćoṅgā, q.v.). s.m. 'A hand-breadth'; a joint of bamboo (closed at one end, used to send letters in, or to keep oil, salt, &c. in); a tube; barrel (of a gun); a funnel; a drill; a cylindrical tin case; — a looped strap, &c.
more: s. m. A joint of bamboo used to send letters in.

def: v.t. To prick, to pierce, to goad ( = ćubhānā).

more: v. a. To scratch, to claw.

A lock of hair left on the top of the head (the rest being shaven off); the hair braided or plaited and hung down behind, a cue; a coil (of hair), a top-knot; an ornament worn on the head (by women); crest (of a bird); top, summit, acme; peak (of a mountain); pinnacle; apex, vertex; the part by which a balance is suspended
subs: 'To rub the top of the head against the sky'; to be very vain; to be very aspiring or ambitious
adj. Having a ćoṭī; having a crest, crested; having a peak; pointed, tapering
To retain a lock of hair on the top of the head (as a votive offering, &c.); — to overcharge

v.n. To excel, be pre-eminent or unrivalled, &c.

def: s.m. = ćuṭlā, q.v. s.m. A cue or lock of hair worn behind; the ribbon with which the hair is tied or braided at the end; the hair so tied or braided ( = ćoṅṭlā); thick large top-knot (of a woman); a coil of false (human) hair (worn by women).
more: s. m. 1. The ribbon with which hair is tied or braided at the end, the cue or lock of hair behind. 2. False hair mixed with the real.

def: s.f. = ćob, q.v. s.f. Wood, timber; pole of a tent, &c.), a post; a staff, mace, club; stick; drumstick
more: s. f. Desire, wish, hope. See چونپ chomp.

more: v. n. To throw water from a daurī , q. v. See ulīchnā .

def: s.m. A thief, a robber, a pilferer; — an unperceived hole (in a roof, wall, &c.); a secret outlet; — an omission, a deficiency; a sore, an ulcer (e.g. zaḵẖam-kā ćor); suspicion, mistrust; — adj. Secret, hidden, masked, sly, treacherous, false
subs: s.m. Land possessed rent-free stealthily or unauthorizedly
s.m. A deceptive frame or body, one whose undressed body is stouter or more muscular than it appears with clothes on
s.m. Water obtained by scraping the surface of sandy soil near a river, &c.
Thieves to fall on or attack, or to break (into a house, &c.)
s.m. A masked guard; vanguard (of an army)

s.m. A receiver of stolen goods ( = thāṅgī; ćor-dhanik)

s.m. The apartments of the concubines of great men
s.m., ćor-manḍalī, s.f. A game played by village children (a pebble is concealed in one of several small heaps of cowdung, and he who finds it takes all the cowdung)
s.m. A thief taken with stolen property ( = ćor-ḍhor)
s.m. Hard grains of pulse which remain whole in the mill, or which remain undissolved in cooking
Grit in the dāl or pulse
s.m. A thief in the candle; a bit of half-burnt wick burning independently and consuming oil
s.m. A great thief; — anybody; nobody; — a ghost or goblin.

s.m. A hole dug for water in the dry sandy bed of a river, a well of water in the dry bed of a river ( = ćohā, ćoʼā); a spring; a rivulet, the channel of a stream after it issues from the hills; — a fragrant paste of four ingredients (see ćʼoā).
more: n. m. H. chūnā ; Sant. joro to leak. 1. A hole dug for water in the sandy bed of a river. 2. A spring ( chashmah ). 1. Kue kā chovā nikas gayā . Rus. 2. Choë kā bhī pāṛīṅ nimṛā, Īsā ḍāl dīyā sūkhā . W. Rus. Wom. song Dried up is every spring, such drought upon the land His will hath brought. 3. A rivulet. 4. The bed of a stream after it issues from the hills. 5. ( choiye ) The husk of wet pulse. 6. A perfume (musk, ambergris , saffron and the juice of the flowers of the Arbor tristis , ground together to a paste). Choyā chandan ho-ke lāgā . Marriage song. Ṭonā. choë kī bāt , n. f. An important observation.
s. m. 1. Husk of grain, the scale of a fish. 2. A hole dug in the sandy bed of a river which has dried up, in which water is found.

Giving a horse new shoes. (See کهول بندي). 2. The fastenings of baggage. چو پاره chau-pāra, adj. Four-pieced. چو پاس chau-pās, adv. All round, around. چو پـﮩل chau-pahal, adj. Four-sided; s. f. s.f. A prick, puncture, stab, sting; spur, motive, incentive.

ety: fr. S. चुच्यावय˚ or चिच्यावय˚, by redupl. fr. rt. च्यु
def: v.n. To drop, trickle, drip, leak, distil, exude ( = ćūnā); to be drenched (with water or grease, &c.), to be dripping wet (with sweat, &c.); to be oozy or miry; to be bursting as though about to exude (fruit, or roses on the cheeks).
more: v. n. To be bursting as though about to exude. (A pomegranate, or the roses on the cheeks).
v. n. To drip; ooze; leak.
v. n. To drop, to be drenched with water, to be dripping wet with sweat, water, &c.

def: s.m. A large breast; a large udder.
s.f. Breast, pap, nipple, teat, dug
more: n. f. S. चूचुक, Pālī, chuchukam . The breast; teat; pap. chūchī-pītā, dūdh pītā, god-kā , n. m. A suckling; a child in arms. chūchī pīnā yā lenā , v. a. To take the breast; to suck. chūchī malnā , v. a. To squeeze the breasts. chūchiyoṅ meṅ hāṛ ṭaṭolnā , v. a. To squeeze a stone; draw blood from stone.
s. f. Breast, pap, nipple, teat, dug. See چونچي chūnchī.
subs: s.m. A sucking child, a child in arms
ćūćī lenā, To suck, to take the breast
'To feel for bones in bubbies'; to squeeze a stone, to draw blood from a stone.

def: s.f. = ćūćī, q.v.
more: W. Rus. n. f. Father's brother's wife; aunt. chachyā-sās , n. f. See pītas . chachyā-susar , n. m. See pītasrā .
s. f. Fatherʼs brotherʼs wife, aunt.
s. (dakh.) Breast (of a woman), pap, dug, teat ( Harris ): see چونچي chūnchī.
wety: Inherited from Proto-New Indo-Aryan *𑀘𑀸𑀘𑁆𑀘 (*cācca) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀇𑀓𑀸- (-ikā-), from onomatopoeic nursery words.

def: v.n. = ćućānā, q.v. v.n. To drop, trickle, drip, leak, distil, exude ( = ćūnā); to be drenched (with water or grease, &c.), to be dripping wet (with sweat, &c.); to be oozy or miry; to be bursting as though about to exude (fruit, or roses on the cheeks).
s.f. A sucking sound with the lips by which animals are called or coaxed ( = ćumkār; cf. ćućkārī).
s.f. Chirping to, coaxing, pacifying, fondling; caress; blandishment.
more: n. f. Cheeping by way of caress.

def: v.t. To coax or cheer by making a sucking or kissing sound with the lips; to chirp to; to kiss or chuck (by way of affection or respect), to fondle, to coax ( = cumkārnā).
more: v. n. S. चुच+कृ 1. To cheep (a child or animal). 2. To kiss anything or chuck as a mark of respect. 3. To stroke ( puchkārnā , 2). Nenā ke chuchkārī kai paṛhābah, ḍāṅ tah jamu hihāri jāët . Tir. Boys should be enticed to learn by kindness. Harshness will only drive them away.
v. n. To fondle, to chirp to, to cheer by making the noise occasioned by drawing in the breath with lips protruded.

def: v.t. To cause to drip; to cause to be drenched, &c.
s.f. = ćūṛā, q.v. s.m. Powder; filings; sawdust; bruised grain (to be chucked into the mouth); a small piece, a fragment
subs: v.t. To break to atoms; to reduce to powder; to bruise, to pulverize

def: s.f. Copulation; prostitution; the wages of prostitution
subs: (-kī), To live by pimping; — s.m. A pimp, a bawd.

def: adj. & s.m. Addicted to venery, libidinous; — a salacious person, qui coitu frequenti fruitur.
more: s. m. A salacious person, qui coitu frequenti fruitur.

def: v.t. Viri amplexibus se submittere (mulier).
more: v. a. Viri amplexibus se submittere ( mulier ). chudak'kaṛ, chudvaiyā , adj. Addicted to illicit intercourse; sensuous.
(trans. of چدنا chudnā ) v. a. Viri amplexibus se submittere (mulier).

def: s.f. Pudendum muliebre.
more: n. f. Pudendum muliebre. Ek shab ham pās rah jātīṅ to kyā andher thā? Kyoṅ jī, kyā kuchh is meṅ ghaṭ jātī chudānī āp kī? Nazīr. chudāī , n. f. The wages of prostitution. Itne kī chudāī nahīṅ jitne ke kapṛe phaṭ gaë . Prov. chudāī-khānā , n. m. A pimp; bawd. chudāī khānā , v. a. To live by pimping. chod-ghar , n. m. A bawdy house; a house of call.

def: s.f. Desire for sexual intercourse, lust, lasciviousness.
more: n. f. S. चुद् to drive in. The sexual passion; furor ; heat; lust. Naṅgī dekh chudās lagī . Prov. Seeing her naked he grew excited.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Desirous of copulating; addicted to or fond of venery, lustful.
more: adj. Desirous of copulating; in heat.
m. adj. Desirous of copulating, lustful.

def: s.f. A whore, harlot, strumpet (used as a term of abuse).
v.n. Feminam subigere.
more: v. n. Fœminam subigere . To suffer sexual intercourse.

def: s.f. The wages of prostitution ( = ćudāʼī).
adj. & s.m. = ćudakkaṛ, q.v. adj. & s.m. Addicted to venery, libidinous; — a salacious person, qui coitu frequenti fruitur.
v.t. = ćudānā, q.v. v.t. Viri amplexibus se submittere (mulier).

ety: rt. of ćugnā+Prk. अओ = S. अ+कः
def: s.m. Food picked up with the beak, pickings ( = ćugan; ćugāʼī).
adj. (f. -ī), Picked, &c.; thin, scanty (as a beard, &c.).
s.f. Picking up, pecking, feeding, grazing; pasturage; pickings (for birds); price or fee paid for pasturage, &c.
more: n. f. 1. Grazing; pasturage. 2. The fee or rent paid for pasturage.

def: s.m. An informer, a spy; a tale-bearer, tell-tale; a back-biter, slanderer
more: n. m. 1. A tell-tale; tale-bearer; informer. Chanā aur chugal muṅh lagā burā . Prov. A taste for fried gram and a taste for tale-bearing are both bad. 2. ( chugal ) A pebble to fill up the hole of a chilam .
chug̠ẖul-ḵẖor, s. m. A telltale, a talebearer, backbiter, informer, spy. چغل خوري chug̠ẖul-ḵẖor-ī, s. f. The act of backbiting, talebearing.

def: s.m. Picking, gathering; pecking, feeding; that which is picked out (from grain, &c.); pickings (for birds); — a gather, a plait or fold (in a garment; — syn. ćunat).
more: n. m. What is picked out from any grain and thrown away.
s. m. A plait or fold in a garment.

def: (caus. of ćugnā), To cause to feed or peck, to take (animals, fowls, &c.) out to pick up food.
more: v. a. Caus. of چگنا q. v.
(caus. of چگنا chugnā ) v. a. To cause to peck.

def: s.m. (Braj) = ćugā, q.v. s.m. Food picked up with the beak, pickings ( = ćugan; ćugāʼī).
s.m. An owl; the small screech-owl.
more: n. m. An owl; the small screech-owl. Qadar ullū kī ullū jāntā hai; Humā ko kab chugad pahchāntā hai? Prov. Owls alone owls' worth can know, not eagle scanned by owl below.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian چغد (čuġd, “owl”).

def: s.m. (dialec.) A small earthen lamp ( = ćirāg).
s.f. Tale-bearing; backbiting, slandering, slander
more: n. f. Tale-bearing; backbiting; telling tales. chuglī khānā , v. a. To tell tales against one.
s. f. Talebearing, backbiting. چغلي کهانا chug̠ẖlī khānā, To backbite, to tell tales, to inform.
subs: (-kī), To tell tales (of), to inform (against); to backbite, to slander.

ety: prob. = ćunnā; or ćug˚ = S. चूर्णि+कृ; cf. ćūṅgnā
def: v.t. To pick up food (with the beak), to pick. peck; to feed
more: v. a. 1. See chunnā . 2. To peck; pick up food with the beak; to feed. chug lenā ; Sant. halaṅ , v. a. 1. To pick up. 2. To choose; cull; select.
v. n. To peck, to pick up food with the beak, to pick up food, to feed. چگ لينا chug-lenā, v. a. To select, choose, cull.
subs: v.t. To pick up; — to gather, cull, select, choose ( = ćunnā); to fold, to plait.

def: Chagatai; a now-extinct Turkic language closely related to Uzbek and Uyghur, characterized by its advanced literary tradition and prestigious status and heavily Persianized form, initially spoken by the Mughals whence the many Chagatai loanwords preserved in Urdu; of or pertaining to Chagatai-speakers or Chagatai people; of or pertaining to Chagatai Khan, Genghis Khan's second son; or his Chagatai Khanate, a Turkicized khanate of Central Asia; a surname, Chughtai, from Chagatai
wety: From Chagatai جغتای (jağatāy), compare Uyghur چاغاتاي (chaghatay) and Persian جغتایی (jaġatâyi). Also known more archaically as تُرکی (Turkī, “Turkic; Chagatai”); what the first Mughal Emperor Babur referred to his language as in his autobiography, the Baburnama.

def: s.m. = ćuqandar, q.v. s.m. Beet-root, beet, Beta vulgaris.
v.t. = ćugānā, q.v. (caus. of ćugnā), To cause to feed or peck, to take (animals, fowls, &c.) out to pick up food.
more: v. a. Caus. of chugnā q. v.

ety: prob. fr. S. चुर्, or चर्व्
def: s.m. A rat; a mouse; — (fig. & colloq.) dry mucus sticking out of the nose
more: s. m. A rat, a mouse. چوهي دان chūhe-dān, s. m. A mouse-trap. چوهي ماريا چوهمار chūhe-mār or chūh-mār, s. m. A sparrow-hawk, cormorant, a mouser
subs: s.m. A rat-trap, a mouse trap
s.f. pl. Bracelets made of bits of gold wire fastened together and projecting like a rat's teeth
'To look for a rat's hole,' to seek any place of refuge to fly to through fear
s.m. A mouser, a sparrow-hawk; a cormorant.
wety: Inherited from Ashokan Prakrit *𑀘𑀽𑀳 (*cūha).

def: s.m. The act of hunting by deceiving game with a stalking horse.
more: s. m. The act of hunting by deceiving game with a stalkinghorse.

def: s.m. The sweeper or scavenger caste in a village; a member of that caste, a village sweeper, a scavenger ( = ćūhṛā; syn. bhaṅgī; ḥalāl-ḵẖor).
s.f. A woman of the sweeper caste; the wife of a sweeper ( = ćūhṛī).
adj. (f. -ī), Warm coloured, deeply coloured, deep red; glowing, bright; flushed.
v.n. To dye a deep colour (especially a deep red); to glow (as colour); to blush (a flower).
s.f. A mouse; a rat.
s.m. Excess, abundance.
s.f. A little mouse, a mouse ( = ćūhī).
s.f. A mouse; a rat.
s.m. = ćūlhā, q.v. v.t. To cast into the fire
more: s. m. A large peg or pin.
subs: s.m. An invitation to a feast given to one member of every household.

def: s.f. = ćūlhī, q.v. v.t. To cast into the fire
subs: s.m. An invitation to a feast given to one member of every household.

def: v.t. = ćūsnā, q.v. v.n. To be sucked, to be absorbed.
s.m. A village sweeper, &c. ( = ćūhāṛ, q.v.)
more: s. m. A Hindū sweeper. See حلال خور ḥalāl-ḵẖor.
subs: s.m. Low caste; a man of low caste
s.f. A kind of song and dance.

def: s.f. = ćūhī, q.v. s.f. A mouse; a rat.
s.f. = ćūhāṛī, q.v. s.f. A woman of the sweeper caste; the wife of a sweeper ( = ćūhṛī).
more: s. f. Wife of a چوهڙا chūhṛā or حلال خور ḥalāl-ḵẖor.

def: s.m. = ćūz, q.v. s.m. A young hawk (under a year old), a hawk that has not hunted (cf. ćūza).
more: n. m. 1. Point; wit; merit. 2. Trick; fraud.

def: s.m. = ćūza, q.v. s.m. A chicken; a young bird; a young hawk or kite ( = ćūz)
subs: s.f. An old woman who keeps a young gallant, an old woman fond of striplings.

def: s.m. = ćūza, q.v. s.m. A chicken; a young bird; a young hawk or kite ( = ćūz)
subs: s.f. An old woman who keeps a young gallant, an old woman fond of striplings.

def: s.m. Sourness, acid; sorrel, Rumex vesicarius, or R. montanus; — a medicine made of boiled lemon-juice and pomegranates.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑀤𑀼𑀓𑁆𑀓 (cadukka), from Sanskrit चतुष्क (catuṣka). Cognate with Bengali চক (cok), Punjabi چَوک (cauk) / ਚੌਕ (cauk), Nepali चोक (cok), Gujarati ચોક (cok), Odia ଚଉକ (cauka).

def: s.f. Omission, oversight, inadvertency; failing, failure; miss; mistake, blunder, error, fault.
more: n. f. See بهول 1. Kshamhu chūk an-jānat kerī, Chahíye bipr ur kirpā ghanerī . Rāmāyan. Pardoned should be what's done unwittingly, In Brahman's breast compassion great should be. 2. Merā bhayā chūkṛāṅ hīyā rī, Kār karat maiṅ bar bar chūkūṅ, Phuṅkā jāt saë jīā rī . Wom. Rus. Song. I am all so forgetful, dear, Whate'er I do it's failure mere, My life's consumed in vain I fear.
n. m. S. चुक्र. 1. French sorrel; rumex montanus. Yeh ām to khaṭṭā chūk hai . 2. A medicine made of boiled lemon juice and pomegranates.

n. m. S. चतुःकोण. 1. The main street or central thoroughfare of a city. 2. A square; courtyard. Hai mere ghar ānand badhāvā, motiyan chauk purāo . Song. chauk pūrnā , v. a. To fill a chauk or square with colored meal, perfumes, sweetmeats, etc. on some occasion of rejoicing. chauk lagnā , v. n. To be opened (a row of shops). chauk-nikās , A tax on goods sold in the market.
s. f. An errour, fault, inadvertency, blunder, mistake, failing, miss.
s. m. 1. Sour, acid. 2. A medicine made of boiled lemon-juice and pomegranates.

s. m. 1. A market. 2. A square of a city. 3. A court-yard. 4. A square place filled, at marriages and on other occasions of rejoicing, with sweetmeats, which after certain ceremonies are distributed. چوک بهرنا يا پورنا chauk-bharnā or -pūrnā, To fill a chauk or square with coloured meal, perfumes, sweetmeats, &c. on some occasion of rejoicing. چوک پٿا chauk-paṭṭā, A seat on which the people sit and eat in the chaukā.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑀤𑀼𑀓𑁆𑀓 (cadukka), from Sanskrit चतुष्क (catuṣka). Cognate with Bengali চক (cok), Punjabi چَوک (cauk) / ਚੌਕ (cauk), Nepali चोक (cok), Gujarati ચોક (cok), Odia ଚଉକ (cauka).

def: s.m. Wild sorrel ( = ćūk, q.v. s.f. Omission, oversight, inadvertency; failing, failure; miss; mistake, blunder, error, fault.
more: n. m. 1. An aggregate of four. 2. A square piece of ground. 3. A portion of ground plastered with earth, cow dung and water for a Hindu's cooking, or for worship. 4. A square stone. 5. The four front teeth. chaukā bartan k .; Sant. jereṛ , v. a. To plaster the ground and wash and scrub the pans. chauk-chaknī, chauk-chandā, chauk-chāṅdnī, ganesh-chauth , n. f. A Hin. festival held on the 4th of the light fortnight in Bhādon when the pupils of Patshalas worship Ganesh .
s. m. A kind of sorrel.
s. m. A kind of earthen pot.
s. m. 1. The space in which Hindūs dress or eat their victuals. 2. A square slate of marble, &c. a square space of ground. 3. The four front teeth. 4. The cube of a measure called بانس bāṅs, q. v.
s. m. Kind of sorrel (Rumex vesicarius: Roxb. ).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑀉𑀓𑁆𑀓 (caükka), from Sanskrit चतुष्क (catuṣka).

def: s.m. A kind of earthen pot ( = ćukkā, ćukkaṛ).
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀘𑀉𑀓𑁆𑀓 (caükka), from Sanskrit चतुष्क (catuṣka).

def: s.f. Settlement, adjustment; agreement, decision ( = ćukautā); — oversight, slip ( = ćūk)
subs: (-ko), To escape (one's) notice; to give (one) the slip.

ety: caus. of ćuknā and ćūknā, q.v.
def: v.t. To finish, complete; to settle or fix (the price or rate of); to settle or adjust (an account, differences, &c.), to arrange, decide; to discharge, to pay (a debt, tax, &c.); to assign, allot
s.m. Beet-root, beet, Beta vulgaris.
more: n. m. Beet; beet-root; Beta vulgaris. Chuqandar ban rahā hai . He has turned as red as beet-root.
s. m. Beet, beet-root. (Beta vulgaris. Arab. سلق silḳ ).

def: s.m. Duty, due, customs' duty.
s.f. Settlement, &c. ( = ćuktī; ćukautā, qq.v.). s.f. Settlement; agreement; decree or sentence (of a court, &c.).
wety: Learned borrowing from Classical Sanskrit चकित (cakita, “trembling; (by extension) frightened, startled, shocked”). By surface analysis, چَکْ (cak, stem of چَکْنا (caknā, “to be surprised, bewildered or amazed”)) + ـِت (-it, “-ed”).

def: s.m. One who settles or fixes (a price, rate, or bargain, &c.); one who is ready or prompt to pay.
more: n. m. 1. One who settles rates, bargains, etc. 2. One ready to pay.

ety: fr. ćukautī = ćukā˚ = ćukāw(nā)+Prk. अत्तिआ = S. आपि+ति+इका
def: s.m. Stipulated hire or wages (of a hireling); contract; compact, bargain, agreement; adjustment, settlement, decision; a task; a fixed rate (of rent, &c.); a deed of acquittance
more: n. m. 1. A fixed rate; a contract; bargain. 2. A settlement or composition of a debt ( adā , 1.) 3. A deed of acquittance. chakautā chukā'nā , v. a. To pay what is due. chakautā chuk'nā , v. n. 1. To be fixed or settled. 2. To be paid up.
s. m. A task, agreement, ad justment, decision, settlement.
subs: To pay a sum stipulated for or agreed upon
The stipulated amount to be paid; a fixed sum or rate to be agreed to, a bargain to be made, to come to terms.

def: s.m. (prov.) = ćukautā, q.v. s.m. Stipulated hire or wages (of a hireling); contract; compact, bargain, agreement; adjustment, settlement, decision; a task; a fixed rate (of rent, &c.); a deed of acquittance
subs: To pay a sum stipulated for or agreed upon
The stipulated amount to be paid; a fixed sum or rate to be agreed to, a bargain to be made, to come to terms.

def: s.m. (prov.) = ćukautā, q.v. s.m. Stipulated hire or wages (of a hireling); contract; compact, bargain, agreement; adjustment, settlement, decision; a task; a fixed rate (of rent, &c.); a deed of acquittance
subs: To pay a sum stipulated for or agreed upon
The stipulated amount to be paid; a fixed sum or rate to be agreed to, a bargain to be made, to come to terms.

def: s.m. Name of a medicine, orris-root, orrice-or iris-root.
v.t. To suck, &c. ( = ćūsnā, q.v.). v.n. To be sucked, to be absorbed.

ety: S. चुलुकः; or चूतकः or चूष+कः; cf. ćukkaṛ
def: s.m. A small earthen pot for drinking spirituous liquours out of ( = ćukkāṛ, ćukkaṛ; syn. kulhiyā).
s.m. = ćukkaṛ (of which it is the Urdū form).
more: s. m. A shallow hole with water in it. See چکڙ chukkaṛ.

def: s.m. The roaring of a lion.
s.m. A small earthen cup ( = ćukkaṛ).
s.m. A small earthen vessel, a drinking-cup (syn. kulhiyā; cf. ćukkāṛ); a pit, a small tank or pond.
s.f. Trickery, deceit, fraud

ety: ćuk˚ = Prk. चुक्क(इ), fr. S. च्युत्+कृ; cf. ćūknā
def: v.n. To be finished, &c. (see ćuk-jānā, the com. form); — ćuk-jānā, v.n. To be finished, be completed; to be exhausted (as a supply or store), to come to an end, to cease ( = ho-ćuknā); to be settled, be fixed, be agreed upon (as a price, &c.); to be settled, be adjusted, be adjudicated (as a difference or dispute, or a law-suit); — to fall short (of), to fail (see ćūknā)
subs: adj. (f. -ī) & s m. Falling short (of), failing; — one who fails (to do anything).
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀘𑀺𑀓𑁆𑀓𑀡 (cikkaṇa) + Middle Indo-Aryan -𑀓- (-ka-), from Sanskrit चिक्कण (cikkaṇa).

ety: ćūk˚ = Prk. चुक्क(इ), fr. S. च्युत्+कृ; cf. ćuknā
def: v.n. To mistake, to blunder, to commit a fault or error; to depart (from), to deviate (from), to go astray, to wander, to err, to miscarry; to be neglectful or deficient (in), to fall short (of), to fail; to fail to hit (a mark), to miss; to be short (of); to be missing; to be inadvertently omitted
more: v. n. See bhūlnā . 1. Kāshī kā maiṅ bāsī kahiye, karam dushā kā hīnā; Rām-bhajan meṅ chūk paṛī, tab pakaṛ julāhā kīnā . Kabīr. A native of Banaras I, luckless my fate, Ram worshipped not aright, a weaver hence create. 2. Ahār chūke voh gaë, beohār chūke voh gaë, Darbār chūke voh gaë, susrāl chūke voh gaë . Prov. Undone who will not eat for shame, nor even press his claim, Undone who fails at Court, of whom his wife's kin can make game.
adj. H. chau four, kān ear. Watchful; on the qui vive ; circumspect. chaukannā h. , v. n. To prick up one's ears; wake up.
v. n. To blunder, to mistake, depart from, miss, err, fail, miscarry.
adj. Cautious, alert, circumspect, sly, on oneʼs guard, wary.

def: s.m. Sourness; acid seasoning; vinegar made by the acetous fermentation of grain; Indian sorrel, Rumex vesicarius.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit चक्र (cakra). Doublet of چرخہ (carxa), چاک (cāk), چکر (cakr), and چَکَّہ (cakka).

def: s.f. A fractional division of land.
s.f. Wood-sorrel, Oxalis monodelpha, or Rumex vesicarius.
s.m. A handful ( = bukṭā).
adj. (f. -ī), Settled, fixed; definite, conclusive; — wholesale.

s.f. A pinch, &c. ( = ćuṭkī, q.v.). s.f. A pinch; a handful; snapping the fingers; a knot in cloth (made by a dyer to prevent a part taking the dye); hammer (of a gun); a twist given to lace; stretching cloth after it is sewn; a portion of grain (about two seers) given to a weighman as his fee; a medicinal powder; a ring worn (by women) on any of the toes (except the great toe)
subs: adv. In the time it takes to snap the fingers, in a moment, in a trice

Chhāyā chhūyā ghar pāyā, aur baṅdhī pāī ṭaṭṭī, Dusre kā janmā laṛkā pāyā, chummā len ki chaṭṭī . Prov. Sāëṅ sāëṅ, sīlī, haldī se pīlī, Chaṭākh chummā le gaī, bahut dukh de gaī . Riddle. Ans. Bhiṛ wasp. Hum hum, his body moist, as turmeric yellow, A kiss he snatches and leaves you to bellow. 2. To lick (Sant. jāl ); fawn upon; cringe or toady to. Ap chūmegā aur le jāëgā. chūmne lāyaq , adj. 1. Inviting a kiss; lovely; loveable. 2.
